1 ^ Domine: 2 ^ Dīrigātur ōrātiō mea sīcut incensum in conspectū tuō~; ēlevātiō manuum meārum sacrificium vespertīnum. ^ Let my prayer be directed as incense in thy sight; the lifting up of my hands

quum clāmāverō ad tē. ^ O Lord: et ōstium circumstantiae labiīs meīs. ^ Set a watch
and a door round about my lips.

1 ^ Domine: 4 ^ Nōn dēclīnēs cor meum in verba malitiae
clāmāvī ad tē: ad excūsandās excūsātiōnēs in peccātīs~; cum hominibus operantibus inīquitātem
exaudī mē~; intende vōcī meae: et nōn commūnicābō cum ēlectīs eōrum. ^ Incline not my heart to evil words~; to make excuses in sins~; with men that work iniquity
quum clāmāverō ad tē. ^ O Lord: and I will not communicate with the choicest of them.

clāmāvī ad tē: et increpābit mē~; oleum autem peccātōris nōn impinguet caput meum
exaudī mē~; intende vōcī meae: quoniam adhūc et ōrātiō mea in benēplacitīs eōrum. ^ The just man shall correct me in mercy
I have cried to thee: but let not the oil of the sinner fatten my head
hear me; hearken to my voice: for my prayer shall still be against the things with which they are well pleased.

1 ^ Domine: 6 ^ Absorptī sunt junctī petrae jūdicēs eōrum~; audient verba mea quoniam potuērunt. ^ Their judges
exaudī mē~; intende vōcī meae: have been swallowed up; They shall hear my words

1 ^ Domine: 7 ^ Sīcut crassitūdō terrae ērupta est super terram
clāmāvī ad tē: dissipāta sunt ossa nostra secus infernum. ^ As when the thickness of the earth is broken up upon the ground
exaudī mē~; intende vōcī meae: our bones are scattered by the side of hell.
Domine oculī meī~; in tē spērāvī
quum clāmāverō ad tē. ^ O Lord: nōn auferās animam meam. ^ But to thee
are my eyes; in thee have I put my trust

exaudī mē~; intende vōcī meae: et ā scandalīs operantium inīquitātem. ^ Keep me from the snare
quum clāmāverō ad tē. ^ O Lord: which they have laid for me
I have cried to thee: and from the stumblingblocks of them that work iniquity.

1 ^ Domine: 10 ^ Cadent in rētiāculō ejus peccātōrēs~; singulāriter sum egō
clāmāvī ad tē: dōnec transeam. ^ The wicked shall fall in his net; I am alone until I pass.
